name: Graham Cracker-Coated Pie Shell
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°.
2. Mix flour, salt, and sugar in a food processor fitted with a steel blade.
3. Scatter the butter pieces over the mixture, tossing to coat the butter with a little flour. Cut the butter into the flour with five one-second pulses.
4. Add the shortening; continue cutting it in until the flour is pale yellow and resembles coarse cornmeal, with butter bits no larger than a small pea (about four more one-second pulses).
5. Turn the mixture into a medium bowl and sprinkle 3 tablespoons water over it. Using a rubber spatula, fold the water into the flour mixture, then repeatedly press down on the dough with the broad side of the spatula until the dough sticks together. Add up to 1 tablespoon more water if the dough will not come together.
6. Shape the dough into a ball with your hands and flatten it into a 4-inch disk. Dust lightly with flour, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 30 minutes before rolling.
7. Generously sprinkle the work area with 2 tablespoons graham crumbs. Place the unwrapped dough on the work area and scatter a few more crumbs over the top. Roll the dough from the center to the edges to make an 9-inch disk, rotating a quarter turn after each stroke and sprinkling additional crumbs underneath and on top as necessary until the dough is heavily coated.
8. Flip the dough and continue rolling it into a 13-inch disk that is just under 1/8 inch thick.
9. Place the dough in a 9-inch Pyrex pie pan. Trim and flute the crust.
10. Refrigerate until firm, about 30 minutes. Prick the shell at half-inch intervals.
11. Press a doubled 12-inch square of aluminum foil inside the pie shell and prick the foil.
12. Refrigerate for at least 30 more minutes to let the dough relax.
13. Reduce the oven temperature to 350°, remove the foil, and continue baking until the crust is crisp and golden brown, about 10-15 minutes longer.
14. Transfer to a wire rack and cool completely.
